text: The pipeline shall support launching data generation with a generate command that accepts a dataset config YAML file.
The pipeline shall support checking generation progress with a status command that accepts a run_id parameter.
The pipeline shall support finalizing a dataset with a finalize command that accepts a run_id parameter.
The pipeline shall create a frozen input specification (input_spec.json) that captures all generation parameters including per-shard seeds, shard shapes, and renderer version.
A shard shall be considered staged-valid when both a .h5 file and a .valid marker exist in the staging directory for that shard.
The generate command shall compute which shards are missing by comparing the spec against staged-valid shards in R2.
The generate command shall partition missing shards across N workers and submit worker tasks to cloud compute.
The generate command shall exit immediately after submitting worker tasks without waiting for completion.
The generate command shall verify R2 and RunPod credentials before launching any workers.
The status command shall report the count of staged-valid, missing, and quarantined shards without loading shard data.
The status command shall be safe to run from any machine at any time without side effects.
The finalize command shall check for a dataset.complete marker to determine if finalization is already done.
The finalize command shall verify all required shards are staged-valid before proceeding with promotion.
The finalize command shall perform structural validation of each staged shard: valid HDF5 format, expected datasets present, and shapes match specification.
The finalize command shall promote validated staged shards to canonical path data/shards/shard-{id}.h5.
The finalize command shall write a .promoted marker for each shard promoted to canonical storage.
The finalize command shall download canonical shards and reshard into train/val/test splits.
The finalize command shall compute normalization statistics (mean and standard deviation) across the training set.
When output_format is hdf5, finalize shall produce HDF5 virtual datasets named train.h5, val.h5, and test.h5.
When output_format is wds, finalize shall produce WebDataset .tar archives for each split.
The finalize command shall write dataset.json containing dataset metadata and shard content hashes.
The finalize command shall register the finalized dataset as a Weights & Biases artifact named data-{dataset_config_id}.
The finalize command shall write dataset.complete marker as the absolute final step of execution.
Workers shall render audio samples through the Surge XT VST plugin.
Workers shall produce HDF5 shards containing audio, mel_spec, and param_array datasets.
Workers shall validate shards locally before upload: verify valid HDF5, expected datasets exist, and row count matches specification.
Workers shall upload validated shards to metadata/workers/shards/shard-{id}/{worker_id}-{attempt_uuid}.h5.
Workers shall write a .rendering marker when starting shard generation.
Workers shall write a .valid marker as the final step after shard validation and upload completes.
Workers shall write a .invalid marker and upload the shard to quarantine/ if validation fails.
Workers shall never write to the data/shards/ prefix; this canonical prefix is reserved exclusively for finalize.
The pipeline shall support local compute mode using Docker containers with local filesystem instead of R2.
The pipeline shall support RunPod backend for cloud execution.
Shard identifiers shall be deterministic logical IDs (shard-000000, shard-000001, etc.) independent of worker infrastructure.
Any worker shall be able to compute any shard; work assignment shall be determined by reconciliation of missing shards.
Concurrent invocations of generate on the same run_id shall be safe and not corrupt data.
Concurrent invocations of finalize on the same run_id shall be safe and not corrupt data.
Running finalize while workers are still uploading shards shall be safe and not corrupt the finalized dataset.
Each shard shall render in a separate operating system process using the spawn context.
A crash (SIGSEGV, OOM) in the VST plugin for one shard shall not affect other shards on the same worker.
Worker process crash shall upload debug logs via bash EXIT trap before terminating.
Missing shards shall be detected via reconciliation regardless of the failure mode.
Content hashes (SHA-256) shall be recorded in worker reports for provenance and divergence detection.
Shards that fail validation shall be uploaded to metadata/workers/shards/shard-{id}/quarantine/ and marked with .invalid.
The pipeline shall record code version (git commit SHA), repository dirty status, parameter specification, and renderer version in the frozen specification.
All pipeline commands (generate, status, finalize) shall be safe to re-run without manual intervention.
The status command shall report which specific shards are missing and their quarantine status.
The pipeline shall not require a database or message queue for coordination; storage shall serve as the coordination layer.
Cloudflare R2 shall serve as both the data plane (shard storage) and control plane (coordination metadata).
Shard completeness shall be determined by file existence and content validation in R2, not by metadata databases.
Worker reports and debug logs shall be auxiliary metadata for debugging; they shall not be authoritative for shard admission decisions.
When multiple staged attempts exist for the same shard, finalize shall select the attempt with the lexicographically smallest filename.
Staging and canonical storage prefixes shall be completely separate; workers shall write only to metadata/workers/, and finalize shall write only to data/.
Zombie worker uploads to the staging prefix after finalize completes shall not corrupt canonical data in data/shards/.
The pipeline shall support dataset scales up to multi-terabyte sizes.
Generation throughput shall achieve at least 500k samples in under 2 hours using 10 workers.
The cost per 500k-sample dataset run shall not exceed $5.
All worker credentials (RunPod API key, R2 credentials, W&B API key) shall be validated before launching any workers.
The pipeline shall provide structured JSONL debug logs from workers with timestamps and structured fields.
The pipeline shall track per-shard errors and results in worker reports.
The pipeline shall preserve quarantined shards indefinitely for post-run debugging and analysis.
Worker reports shall record CPU architecture and OS information to detect non-determinism across heterogeneous hardware.
The input specification shall be frozen and immutable after initial creation.
Running generate multiple times on the same run_id shall only re-generate shards that are missing or invalid.
The finalize command shall be idempotent; re-running finalize shall produce identical results without data corruption.
Shard filenames in staging shall include {worker_id}-{attempt_uuid} to uniquely identify each generation attempt.
Lifecycle markers (.rendering, .valid, .invalid, .promoted) shall be empty files where presence indicates state.
Finalize shall not delete staged files; the staging directory shall preserve the complete history of all attempts.
The pipeline shall detect and report when non-deterministic outputs occur across heterogeneous hardware.
Per-shard process isolation shall use the spawn context to create a fresh Python interpreter for each shard.
Each shard generation shall have a configurable timeout.
Worker reports shall include per-shard results, content hashes, timing information, and error details.
Worker debug logs shall be in JSONL format with ISO 8601 timestamps and structured event fields.
The dataset.json card shall include provenance information, dataset structure, statistics, and validation summary.
The dataset.complete marker shall contain the run_id and finalization timestamp.
W&B artifacts shall be named data-{dataset_config_id} with type dataset.
W&B artifact metadata shall include dataset_config_id, shard_count, param_spec, and code_version.
W&B artifacts shall reference the R2 path rather than uploading the actual HDF5 shard data.
Training runs shall declare finalized datasets as inputs using W&B artifact lineage.
The pipeline shall support two output formats: hdf5 and wds (WebDataset).
Output format shall be specified in the config and frozen in the specification at run creation.
Workers shall always produce HDF5 shards regardless of the target output format for training.
Finalize shall perform format conversion from HDF5 staging format to the specified output format.
WebDataset .tar archives shall contain individual samples as .audio.npy, .params.npy, and .mel.npy files.
HDF5 output shall use virtual datasets referencing the canonical promoted shards.
The config filename without extension shall serve as the dataset_config_id.
The dataset_wandb_run_id shall follow the format {dataset_config_id}-{YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ}.
The R2 storage path shall follow the format data/{dataset_config_id}/{dataset_wandb_run_id}/.
Per-shard seeds shall be derived deterministically as base_seed + shard_id.
Re-running generation with the same frozen specification shall produce an identical dataset.
The VST plugin version shall be automatically extracted and recorded in the specification.
The git commit SHA shall be recorded in the specification for reproducibility tracking.
The pipeline shall preserve the complete history of all shard generation attempts in the staging directory.
Multiple generation attempts for the same shard shall be visible in the staging directory with distinct filenames.
The .valid marker shall be authoritative for staging admission but not sufficient for final dataset correctness.
The finalize command shall be the authoritative gate for data promotion to canonical storage.
Shard validation shall check for NaN and Inf values in array data (design target feature).
Shard validation shall check that array value bounds conform to parameter specification (design target feature).
Finalize structural validation shall verify HDF5 file validity before promotion.
Finalize shall record content hashes in dataset.json for integrity verification and divergence detection.
The pipeline shall calculate train/val/test dataset splits based on shard counts from the specification.
The pipeline shall compute per-channel statistics during finalization.
Normalization statistics shall be stored in stats.npz format.
Make targets shall serve as convenient aliases for the core pipeline commands.
The status command shall identify orphaned .rendering markers that lack corresponding .valid markers.
Missing shards shall be assigned to workers on each generate invocation based on current staging state.
The generate command shall accept a --config flag specifying the dataset configuration for the first run.
The status and finalize commands shall accept a --run-id flag to operate on existing runs.
Retry invocations shall not require the config flag; the stored specification shall be authoritative.
Worker reports shall record content_hash as the SHA-256 hash of the complete .h5 shard file.
The pipeline shall provide dry-run modes for cost estimation without executing actual work.
Finalize shall produce a dataset card (dataset.json) that describes the final output and is self-describing.
Cloudflare R2 shall provide strong read-after-write consistency guarantees for the pipeline.
Concurrent worker writes to the staging prefix shall not overwrite due to unique per-attempt filenames.
The pipeline shall handle worker timeouts gracefully without corrupting shard state.
The pipeline shall not poll RunPod APIs for task status; completion shall be determined solely by reconciliation of R2 storage.
The pipeline shall support credential validation via environment variables.
The pipeline shall bake credentials into Docker images via BuildKit secrets.
When the repository is dirty, finalize shall create and upload a git diff patch for provenance.
The evaluation pipeline must consist of three sequential stages: predict, render, and metrics.
The evaluation pipeline must run on macOS without code modifications.
The evaluation pipeline must run on Linux without code modifications.
The evaluation pipeline must run in Docker containers without code modifications.
The evaluation pipeline must run in GitHub Actions CI runners without code modifications.
Committed configuration files must not contain /data/scratch/ paths.
Dataset paths must follow the pattern ${paths.data_dir}/{dataset_config_id}/{dataset_wandb_run_id}.
Checkpoint resolution must support ${wandb:...} OmegaConf resolver syntax.
Downloaded W&B checkpoints must be cached in $PROJECT_ROOT/.cache/checkpoints/.
Cached W&B checkpoints must be reused on subsequent runs if checksums match.
All rclone operations must include the --checksum flag.
R2 dataset downloads are opt-in via explicit r2_path specification with no default value.
Eval artifact R2 paths must follow eval/{dataset_config_id}/{dataset_wandb_run_id}/{train_config_id}/{train_wandb_run_id}/{eval_config_id}/{eval_wandb_run_id}/ format.
The make target 'predict' must execute the predict stage.
The make target 'render' must execute the render stage.
The make target 'metrics' must execute the metrics stage.
The make target 'eval' must execute predict, render, and metrics stages sequentially.
The make target 'docker-eval' must execute the full pipeline in a Docker container.
The make target 'upload-eval' must upload eval artifacts to R2 with --checksum verification.
Display server detection must use native display on macOS.
Display server detection must automatically launch Xvfb on headless Linux.
Display server detection must configure DISPLAY environment variable appropriately for the platform.
The default VST plugin path must be plugins/Surge XT.vst3.
The default preset path must be presets/surge-base.vstpreset.
Checkpoint path must be required in eval.yaml with no default value.
Checkpoint path must be optional in train.yaml with default value null.
The metrics stage must compute MSS (Multi-Scale Spectrogram) metric.
The metrics stage must compute wMFCC (Weighted MFCC) metric.
The metrics stage must compute SOT (Spectral Optimal Transport) metric.
The metrics stage must compute RMS (RMS Amplitude Envelope) metric.
Metrics output must include per-sample metrics in metrics.csv.
Metrics output must include aggregated statistics (mean and standard deviation) in aggregated_metrics.csv.
W&B eval runs must declare input artifacts via use_artifact() method.
W&B eval runs must log summary metrics for MSS, wMFCC, SOT, and RMS.
All make targets must be idempotent and safe to re-run.
The render stage must skip samples that already exist on disk.
Metric computation must only recompute when input files change.
Fresh clone to metrics.csv completion must take less than 15 minutes for a small fixture.
SGE scripts in jobs/predict/ are deprecated and must not be maintained.
Configuration file surge_simple.yaml must be updated to remove /data/scratch/ paths.
Configuration file surge_mini.yaml must be updated to remove /data/scratch/ paths.
A rclone wrapper utility must be created at src/data/rclone.py.
The rclone wrapper must enforce --checksum flag in all sync operations.
SurgeDataModule.prepare_data() must sync datasets from R2 when r2_path is explicitly specified.
The ${wandb:...} OmegaConf resolver must be implemented in src/utils/utils.py.
CI workflows must not require R2 credentials.
CI workflows must not require W&B credentials.
Test fixtures must include a minimal checkpoint file.
Test fixtures must include a minimal dataset.
Test fixtures must include sample audio files for testing.
Every evaluation run is traceable from metrics CSV to checkpoint to training run to dataset.
Predict stage outputs must include pred-{batch_idx}.pt files.
Predict stage outputs must include target-audio-{batch_idx}.pt files.
Predict stage outputs must include target-params-{batch_idx}.pt files.
Render stage outputs must include sample_{N}/pred.wav files.
Render stage outputs must include sample_{N}/target.wav files.
Render stage outputs must include sample_{N}/spec.png files.
Render stage outputs must include sample_{N}/params.csv files.
The predict stage must accept ckpt_path parameter for checkpoint location.
The predict stage must accept data parameter for dataset configuration.
The predict stage must accept experiment parameter for experiment configuration.
The render stage must accept an input directory containing .pt prediction files.
The render stage must accept an output directory for audio and spectrogram outputs.
The render stage must accept --plugin_path parameter to override VST plugin location.
The render stage must accept --preset_path parameter to override preset location.
The metrics stage must accept an input directory containing sample_{N}/ subdirectories with .wav files.
The metrics stage must accept an output directory for CSV outputs.
`dataset_config_id` is constructed from the config filename stem stored at `configs/dataset/{id}.yaml`
`dataset_wandb_run_id` defaults to `{dataset_config_id}-{YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ}` format unless explicitly configured
`train_config_id` is constructed from the config filename stem stored at `configs/experiment/.../{id}.yaml`
`train_wandb_run_id` defaults to `{train_config_id}-{YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ}` format unless explicitly configured
`eval_config_id` is constructed from the eval dataset config filename stem stored at `configs/dataset/{id}.yaml`
`eval_wandb_run_id` defaults to `{eval_config_id}-{YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ}` format unless explicitly configured
Default timestamp format for W&B run IDs is `YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ` (UTC, seconds precision)
W&B run ID maximum length is 64 characters
R2 data storage path is `intermediate-data/data/{dataset_config_id}/{dataset_wandb_run_id}/`
R2 training storage path is `intermediate-data/train/{dataset_config_id}/{dataset_wandb_run_id}/{train_config_id}/{train_wandb_run_id}/`
R2 evaluation storage path is `intermediate-data/eval/{dataset_config_id}/{dataset_wandb_run_id}/{train_config_id}/{train_wandb_run_id}/{eval_config_id}/{eval_wandb_run_id}/`
Data generation output includes subdirectories: `shards/`, `metadata/`, and files `train.h5`, `val.h5`, `test.h5`, `stats.npz`
Metadata subdirectory contains `config.yaml`, `input_spec.json`, `dataset.json`, `dataset.complete`, and `workers/`
Workers may only write to `metadata/workers/` subdirectory in data generation output
`shards/` directory is written exclusively by the finalize step
All rclone operations use the `--checksum` flag
Datasets are immutable once `dataset.complete` marker file exists
Creating a new version of a dataset requires generating a new `dataset_wandb_run_id`
Training output includes `checkpoints/` subdirectory containing `last.ckpt` and `best.ckpt` files
Training output includes `config.yaml` file with frozen experiment configuration
W&B Lightning Logger must be configured with `log_model="all"` to immediately persist checkpoints as W&B artifacts
Evaluation output includes `predictions/`, `audio/`, and `metrics/` subdirectories
`predictions/` subdirectory contains `pred-{batch_idx}.pt`, `target-audio-{batch_idx}.pt`, and `target-params-{batch_idx}.pt` files
`audio/{sample_N}/` subdirectory contains `pred.wav`, `target.wav`, `spec.png`, and `params.csv`
`metrics/` subdirectory contains `metrics.csv` and `aggregated_metrics.csv` files
W&B `dataset` artifact type uses naming pattern `data-{dataset_config_id}`
W&B `model` artifact type uses naming pattern `model-{train_config_id}`
W&B `eval-results` artifact type uses naming pattern `eval-{eval_config_id}`
W&B automatically versions artifacts with sequential suffixes (`:v0`, `:v1`, `:v2`, etc.)
`*_wandb_run_id` values are stored in W&B artifact metadata
W&B automatically sets `:latest` alias on every artifact log
Training script sets `:best` alias on model artifact when validation metric improves
Promote workflow sets `:production` alias on model artifact when promoting to GitHub Release
Every run must call `run.use_artifact()` to consume input artifacts and create lineage links
Every run must call `run.log_artifact()` to produce output artifacts
Every run must include `github_sha` in `wandb.config`
`wandb.config` stores hyperparameters and settings configured before the run
`wandb.summary` stores final metrics measured after the run completes
`artifact.metadata` stores properties of the artifact itself (separate from config/summary)
Dataset properties are stored on the dataset artifact, not on downstream training runs that consume it
`job_type=data-generation` is set for data generation pipeline runs
`job_type=training` is set for training stage runs
`job_type=evaluation` is set for evaluation stage runs
GitHub Actions Tests workflow (test.yml) triggers on push and pull request events
GitHub Actions Tests workflow runs on `ubuntu-latest` and `macos-latest` runners
GitHub Actions Full Tests workflow (test-expensive.yml) triggers on schedule and dispatch events
GitHub Actions Full Tests workflow runs on `gpu-x64` runner
GitHub Actions Data Generation workflow (dataset-generation.yml) triggers on `workflow_call` event
GitHub Actions Data Generation workflow runs on `ubuntu-latest-4core` runner
GitHub Actions Data Generation workflow inputs include `image_tag`, `config_path`, and `artifact_name`
GitHub Actions Training workflow is TBD
GitHub Actions Evaluation workflow is TBD
GitHub Actions Model Promotion workflow (promote.yml) is planned
GitHub Actions Model Promotion workflow triggers on `workflow_dispatch` event
GitHub Actions Model Promotion workflow runs on `ubuntu-latest` runner
GitHub Actions Model Promotion workflow inputs include `train_wandb_run_id`, `eval_wandb_run_id`, `registry`, and `dry_run`
All workflows that create W&B runs must export `GITHUB_SHA` environment variable
Evaluation workflow requires `train_wandb_run_id` as input to locate the model artifact
Evaluation workflow requires `eval_config_id` as input to select the evaluation dataset
Model Promotion workflow requires `train_wandb_run_id` to pull the model artifact
Model Promotion workflow requires `eval_wandb_run_id` to pull evaluation metrics
GitHub Release tag format is `model-v{N}` where N is an incrementing integer
GitHub Release includes model file asset in `.pt` or `.onnx` format
GitHub Release body includes link to W&B training run
GitHub Release body includes link to W&B evaluation run
GitHub Release body includes UTC timestamp of release
GitHub Release body includes Git SHA of the commit that produced the training run
GitHub Release body includes training metrics from training run's `wandb.summary`, excluding underscore-prefixed metrics
GitHub Release body includes evaluation metrics from eval run's `wandb.summary`, excluding underscore-prefixed metrics
GitHub Release body includes full training configuration as JSON
GitHub Release body includes dataset artifacts used by training run with versions
GitHub Release body includes evaluation dataset artifacts used by eval run with versions
`WANDB_API_KEY` secret is used by data generation, training, evaluation, and promotion workflows
`WANDB_API_KEY` is sourced from wandb.ai/settings
`GITHUB_TOKEN` secret is used by promotion workflow
`GITHUB_TOKEN` is automatically available in GitHub Actions
`RUNPOD_API_KEY` secret is used by data generation and training workflows
`RUNPOD_API_KEY` is sourced from runpod.io
`RCLONE_CONFIG_R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID` secret is used by data generation, training, and evaluation workflows
`RCLONE_CONFIG_R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID` is sourced from Cloudflare R2 dashboard
`RCLONE_CONFIG_R2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY` secret is used by data generation, training, and evaluation workflows
`RCLONE_CONFIG_R2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY` is sourced from Cloudflare R2 dashboard
`RCLONE_CONFIG_R2_ENDPOINT` secret is used by data generation, training, and evaluation workflows
`RCLONE_CONFIG_R2_ENDPOINT` is sourced from Cloudflare R2 dashboard
Secrets must only be available to workflows that require them
Training workflow requires R2 read access to download dataset shards
Training workflow requires R2 write access to upload checkpoints
W&B entity is configured as `tinaudio`
W&B project is configured as `synth-setter`
W&B entity and project are set via `WANDB_ENTITY` and `WANDB_PROJECT` environment variables
Configuration files use `entity: ${oc.env:WANDB_ENTITY,tinaudio}` and `project: ${oc.env:WANDB_PROJECT,synth-setter}` to provide defaults
Legacy runs in `benhayes`/`synth-permutations` W&B workspace remain read-only
W&B artifacts reference R2 objects using `artifact.add_reference("s3://intermediate-data/...")`
R2 is accessed as S3-compatible storage
`AWS_ENDPOINT_URL` or `WANDB_S3_ENDPOINT_URL` environment variable must be set to R2 endpoint in environments calling `add_reference` or downloading reference artifacts
Without R2 endpoint configuration, W&B attempts to resolve artifacts against AWS S3
W&B artifacts contain metadata, manifests, and statistics but not bulk data files
Bulk data is stored in R2, while W&B provides the index and lineage graph
`*_wandb_run_id` values uniquely identify immutable run outputs
Every run must log W&B artifacts for its outputs
Every run must consume W&B artifacts for its inputs
All configuration files are frozen into artifacts or R2 storage paths at run time
R2 storage paths are append-only after completion markers are created
Runs must not consume data from R2 paths that lack a completion marker
Training must execute successfully on local Linux machines
Training must execute successfully on local macOS machines
Training must execute successfully in Docker containers
Training must execute successfully on RunPod cloud infrastructure
Training command must accept `experiment=` parameter for specifying experiment configuration
Training command must accept config overrides via command-line parameters
Training must output metrics to W&B
Training must output lineage information to W&B
Training must save `last.ckpt` checkpoint to local disk
Training must save `best.ckpt` checkpoint to local disk
WandbLogger configured with `log_model="all"` must upload every checkpoint to W&B immediately after save
Resume via `ckpt_path=<local_path>` must restore optimizer state
Resume via `ckpt_path=<local_path>` must restore scheduler state
Resume via `ckpt_path=wandb:<artifact>` must download W&B artifact and restore training
Every training run must set `job_type="training"` in W&B configuration
Every training run must call `run.use_artifact()` for the dataset artifact
Every training run must call `run.log_artifact()` for the model artifact
Every training run must include `github_sha` in `wandb.config`
Model artifacts must follow naming pattern `model-{train_config_id}`
Dataset artifacts must follow naming pattern `data-{dataset_config_id}`
Training must write final metrics to `wandb.summary`
Dataset paths must follow convention `${paths.data_dir}/{dataset_config_id}/{dataset_wandb_run_id}`
Training must not fetch datasets from R2 by default without explicit configuration
When `r2_path` is specified in dataset config, training must sync dataset from R2 before training begins
macOS development environment must support Metal Performance Shaders (MPS) GPU acceleration
Linux development environment must support CUDA GPU acceleration
Docker training must support CUDA GPU acceleration
RunPod training must support CUDA GPU acceleration
RunPod training must retrieve datasets from R2
Local development must support local dataset source
Local development must support R2 dataset source
CI training must use fixture datasets
Training must use single long-running job execution model without distributed worker graphs
Training checkpoint durability must rely on W&B artifacts
Resume behavior must use Lightning's native checkpoint loading semantics
Training storage and provenance conventions must align with data pipeline conventions
Model promotion must be handled by separate promotion workflow, not training workflow
Pod death must result in loss of at most one checkpoint interval (5000 steps) of training progress
Intermediate step checkpoints must be uploaded to W&B per checkpoint configuration
Resume from W&B artifacts must be accomplished via a single documented command
Training must not implement reconciliation layer for checkpoint recovery
W&B-only checkpoint strategy must be used in version 1 (R2 mirror deferred)
The entrypoint script (scripts/docker_entrypoint.sh) dispatches on the MODE environment variable.
The container exits with error code 1 if MODE environment variable is unset or empty.
The container exits with error code 1 if MODE is set to an unknown value.
When MODE=idle, the entrypoint executes 'sleep infinity'.
When MODE=passthrough with arguments, the entrypoint executes those arguments ($@).
When MODE=passthrough with no arguments, the entrypoint exits with code 0.
When MODE=generate_dataset, the entrypoint runs VST dataset generation via pipeline.entrypoints.generate_dataset under headless X11.
The docker/ubuntu22_04/Dockerfile defines a single target named dev-snapshot.
The dev-snapshot target uses docker_entrypoint.sh as its entrypoint.
The dev-snapshot target clones source code from a Git reference specified at build time.
The dev-snapshot target is used for CI and cloud run use cases.
The dev-snapshot target inherits from r2-config-base.
R2 credentials are baked into the image only when BuildKit secrets are provided at build time.
A placeholder rclone configuration is used when BuildKit secrets are not provided at build time.
W&B authentication is baked into ~/.netrc when the wandb_api_key BuildKit secret is provided at build time.
If wandb_api_key BuildKit secret is missing, WANDB_API_KEY must be provided at runtime.
Build ARG IMAGE defaults to dev-snapshot.
Build ARG SYNTH_PERMUTATIONS_GIT_REF defaults to main.
Build ARG SURGE_GIT_REF defaults to a pinned SHA.
Build ARG BUILD_MODE defaults to source.
Build ARG BUILD_MODE accepts either source or prebuilt.
Build ARG R2_BUCKET defaults to empty.
Build ARG TORCH_INDEX_URL is required.
Environment variable SYNTH_PERMUTATIONS_GIT_REF is baked into the dev-snapshot target with the Git reference used at build time.
Environment variable R2_BUCKET is baked into the dev-snapshot target via r2-config-base.
Environment variable VIRTUAL_ENV is set to /venv/main in the dev-snapshot target.
Environment variable PATH is set to include $VIRTUAL_ENV/bin in the dev-snapshot target.
DATASET_CONFIG environment variable is required for MODE=generate_dataset.
DATASET_CONFIG environment variable specifies a path to a dataset config YAML file in the container.
RUN_METADATA_DIR environment variable is optional for MODE=generate_dataset.
RUN_METADATA_DIR environment variable defaults to /run-metadata.
RUN_METADATA_DIR environment variable specifies the directory where input_spec.json is written.
The generate_dataset mode materializes a DatasetPipelineSpec.
The generate_dataset mode uploads the spec and shard to R2.
The generate_dataset mode writes input_spec.json to RUN_METADATA_DIR.
The generate_dataset mode generates shard_size samples per invocation.
The generate_dataset mode raises NotImplementedError when num_shards is greater than 1.
CI workflows currently use --entrypoint bash, which bypasses the docker_entrypoint.sh script.
BATS entrypoint tests are not currently integrated into the CI pipeline.
